RawBacklogText: GET /api/v3/status status contract for both V-SDC and E-SDC; V-SDC uses client certificate and PAC header while E-SDC does not require client authentication.
Notes: Seeded from exact spec lines

RawBacklogText: Status response field set includes isPinRequired auditRequired sdcDateTime lastInvoiceNumber protocolVersion secureElementVersion hardwareVersion softwareVersion deviceSerialNumber make model mssc gsc supportedLanguages uid taxCoreApi currentTaxRates and allTaxRates.
Notes: Keep as a raw cluster until second-pass normalization

RawBacklogText: auditRequired becomes true when total signed amount reaches 75 percent or more of the secure-element maximum limit obtained through the Amount Status APDU command.
Notes: Needs reconciliation with manual-test behavior later

RawBacklogText: POST /api/v3/pin E-SDC-only contract with plain-text PIN request body and plain-text general status code response body.
Notes: The service description is explicit about transport shape

RawBacklogText: On 0100 the E-SDC remembers the PIN in volatile memory until the card is removed or the E-SDC is switched off; if POS later receives 1500 it must ask for PIN again.
Notes: Important ESIR-LPFR session behavior

RawBacklogText: VerifyPin request body must contain only 4 ASCII digits 0-9; invalid format maps to status 2806.
Notes: This is stricter than a generic 4-character constraint

RawBacklogText: VerifyPin-specific response codes listed here are 0100 2100 2110 1300 2220 2210 2230 2400 2806 and 1999.
Notes: Keep as a raw status set before regrouping by code system

RawBacklogText: CreateInvoice request headers are Accept application/json Content-Type application/json optional RequestId with max length 32 Accept-Language with supportedLanguages/406 semantics and PAC for V-SDC.
Notes: Important transport-level detail

RawBacklogText: Model block lists InvoiceRequest plus nested Payment options and Item model shapes including enum/string duality and option flags omitQRCodeGen and omitTextualRepresentation.
Notes: Second pass should split this into types fields and enum/value constraints

RawBacklogText: Payment type numeric mapping is 0 Other 1 Cash 2 Card 3 Check 4 WireTransfer 5 Voucher 6 MobileMoney.
Notes: Confirms numeric representation beside string forms

RawBacklogText: CreateInvoice response field set includes requestedBy signedBy sdcDateTime invoiceCounter invoiceCounterExtension invoiceNumber verificationUrl verificationQRCode journal totalCounter transactionTypeCounter totalAmount encryptedInternalData signature taxItems businessName locationName address tin district taxGroupRevision mrc messages and TaxItem fields label categoryName categoryType rate amount.
Notes: Second pass should split InvoiceResult TaxItem and response-only rule candidates

RawBacklogText: MRC is mandatory in audit packages sent to the tax authority but optional in the invoice response returned to POS.
Notes: Second pass should attach to journal/audit boundaries

RawBacklogText: GET /api/v3/invoices/{requestId} returns the same response shape as CreateInvoice or null if not found; used when POS did not receive a response from CreateInvoice.
Notes: Recovery contract

RawBacklogText: If multiple POS devices share one E-SDC RequestId must be unique at the shared scope; GUID or POS_id plus request_id is recommended; last responses should be stored per request_id plus secure element UID in non-volatile memory at least until the next request.
Notes: High-value operational guidance

RawBacklogText: Error format is ModelErrors with message and modelState; ModelError has property and errors; property paths include array indexes in square brackets when needed.
Notes: Useful for ESIR validation UX

RawBacklogText: PFX certificate password is always an 8-character uppercase alphanumeric code and PAC is always a 6-character uppercase alphanumeric code.
Notes: Stable glossary primitives

RawBacklogText: Verification URL is a standard component of every fiscal invoice, generated by SDC, returned in the response and rendered as QR code on printed receipts or as a hyperlink in electronic delivery.
Notes: Strong conceptual rule with rendering impact

RawBacklogText: Smart card PIN is always 4 digits; after valid entry it need not be re-entered until card removal; E-SDC exposes JSON-based protocols to POS and stores audit data in internal memory for local and remote audits.
Notes: Contains both auth and offline-audit semantics

RawBacklogText: MRC consists of ProductCode ProductVersionCode and DeviceSerialNumber; it is mandatory in audit packages and optional in fiscalized data returned to POS; DeviceSerialNumber may contain special characters up to length 32.
Notes: Use this source together with the protocol spec

RawBacklogText: Invoice verification can be called by GET on the verification URL with Accept application/json and no authentication.
Notes: This is a public machine-readable contract even though it is documented in the applications guide

RawBacklogText: Verification JSON response contains invoiceRequest invoiceResult journal and isValid; nested request/result objects expose POS time taxpayer/location/buyer/payment data and fiscalization counters plus invoice number and signedBy.
Notes: Second pass should split nested view types and fields

RawBacklogText: Applications guide maps invoiceType as 0 Promet 1 Predračun 2 Kopija 3 Obuka 4 Avans; transactionType as 0 Prodaja 1 Refundacija; paymentType as 0 Drugo bezgotovinsko 1 Gotovina 2 Platna kartica 3 Ček 4 Prenos na račun 5 Voucher 6 Instant plaćanje.
Notes: Useful numeric crosswalk beside protocol string names

RawBacklogText: Reference client loads a certificate from StoreName.My StoreLocation.CurrentUser by subject CN, sends PAC header and optional Accept-Language and RequestId headers, then POSTs to /api/v3/invoices.
Notes: Reference-only evidence that supports transport interpretation

RawBacklogText: Reference certificate library enumerates certificate classes V31 through V38 and OID suffixes 3.1 through 3.8 including auth signing encryption and developer variants.
Notes: Reference adapter evidence

RawBacklogText: Reference certificate helper exposes metadata such as UniqueIdentifier CommonName RequestedBy Organization ExpiryDate CertificateType GivenName SurName OrganizationUnit StreetAddress State role flags plus ExtractTIN and ExtractTaxCoreApiUrl.
Notes: Do not elevate above official protocol without corroboration

RawBacklogText: Reference smart-card adapter exposes SelectPKIApp SelectSEApp GetSECert GetPKICert VerifyPkiPin VerifySEPin AmountStatus GetExportInternalData SECommand and FinishAudit with concrete APDU bytes.
Notes: Good source for adapter boundaries and byte-level examples

CandidateLabel: PKI and SE PIN result mapping
RawBacklogText: Reference card reader maps PKI 0x6983 to locked PKI applet, PKI SW1 0x63 to remaining tries in SW2, and SE 0x63 0x10 to locked SE applet.
Notes: Useful for adapter UX and status translation

RawBacklogText: Reference UI subtracts 192 from PkiTrysLeft to display remaining attempts and distinguishes combined card-return messaging from SE-only and PKI-only locked states.
Notes: UX mapping evidence rather than protocol truth

RawBacklogText: Reference Android host validates referentDocumentNumber with regex ^[0-9a-zA-Z]{8}-[0-9a-zA-Z]{8}-[0-9]+$.
Notes: Useful as implementation evidence for the reference-number format

RawBacklogText: Reference Android host still writes wrapper-only top-level paymentType and hash fields while also filling the canonical payment array and core invoice fields.
Notes: Keep separate from canonical request schema

RawBacklogText: Reference host persists invoice response fields such as sdcDateTime totalAmount journal QR invoiceNumber requestedBy counters verificationUrl signedBy encryptedInternalData signature taxGroupRevision plus buyer and payment context.
Notes: Important for later local-journal design

RawBacklogText: Reference host persists country logo taxCoreApi environment names uid and endpoint-derived data from environment-parameters and status responses.
Notes: Useful for host/application configuration planning

RawBacklogText: Every new product version that affects the appearance of the fiscal receipt requires a new approval.
Notes: Important approval-control rule from the technical instruction

RawBacklogText: Receipt must display buyerId when required; optional buyer field is used only in the same kind of identified-buyer transaction and is shown only when needed.
Notes: High-value rendering and scope rule

CandidateLabel: Referent number rendering and fallback forms
RawBacklogText: Receipt must display referent number on Copy and Refund and on Normal Sale linked to Advance Refund; value normally uses the PFR-number format and the instruction also documents fallback placeholder forms for pre-EFU bookkeeping and old fiscal cash registers.
Notes: Needs second-pass split into baseline rule and legacy-fallback examples

RawBacklogText: Copy Proforma and Training documents must contain the message OVO NIJE FISKALNI RACUN and the text must be clearly visible with font at least double the size of the rest of the document text.
Notes: This sharpens the already seeded non-fiscal rendering rule

CandidateLabel: QR versus hyperlink and size rule
RawBacklogText: QR code contains the verification URL internal data and digital signature; if receipt is delivered electronically the QR code is replaced by a hyperlink URL; printed QR size must be at least 40x40 mm and at most 50x50 mm.
Notes: This is stronger than the looser conceptual statement

RawBacklogText: LPFR must validate request structure calculate tax from active tax rates round sums to 4 decimals using half-round-up send fiscalization data with current date time and PIN to the secure element receive the signed invoice create a journal file generate the verification URL return fiscalized data to ESIR and display errors as prescribed by the technical guide.
Notes: Second pass should split these into separate validator/algorithm/journal/transport rules

CandidateLabel: LPFR forbidden functions and PIN storage
RawBacklogText: LPFR must not return arbitrary data on error beyond defined error codes unless documented, must reject nonexistent or inactive tax rates, must not allow protocol-parameter changes, must not store PIN anywhere except working memory, and after restart the operator must re-enter the PIN.
Notes: Contains several negative constraints that should become separate rules later

CandidateLabel: Refund cancellation example with seller TIN as buyerId
RawBacklogText: The worked example shows buyerId 10:111111111 for the seller taxpayer and referentDocumentNumber plus referentDocumentDT for the invoice being canceled.
Notes: Useful for the refund/cancellation branch in the second pass

CandidateLabel: Card removal and PIN reset behavior
RawBacklogText: Manual test requires LPFR to stop operating as soon as the smart card is removed and to require a fresh valid PIN after the card is reinserted.
Notes: Operational expectation source

RawBacklogText: Without a card inserted the manual test expects /api/v3/environment-parameters -> 1300 [400], /api/v3/status -> gsc 1300 1500 0210 [200], /api/v3/pin -> 1300 [200], /api/v3/invoices -> 1300 [400], and /api/v3/invoices/{requestId} -> 1300 [400].
Notes: Important operational matrix

RawBacklogText: Manual testing document includes a worked CreateInvoice payload with buyerId buyerCostCenterId reference number options omitQRCodeGen and omitTextualRepresentation and a Unicode item example.
Notes: Useful as a source-backed request example

RawBacklogText: With a card inserted but no valid PIN the manual test expects /api/v3/status -> gsc 1500 0210 [200], /api/v3/invoices -> 1500, and /api/v3/pin with a wrong PIN -> 2100 [423]; after valid PIN entry /api/v3/pin -> 0100 [200] and /api/v3/status -> 0100 [200].
Notes: High-value operational matrix for ESIR-LPFR integration handling

RawBacklogText: Law defines fiscalization retail invoice advance payment secure processor fiscal invoice electronic fiscal device tax core API ESIR PFR secure element and related foundational terms used across the system.
Notes: Keep as raw legal glossary material for second-pass domain normalization

RawBacklogText: Fiscalization applies to every retail sale of goods and services and to every received advance payment for future retail turnover.
Notes: Top-level domain boundary

RawBacklogText: Every transaction that falls under fiscalization must be recorded regardless of whether payment is cash card transfer voucher instant transfer or any other payment method.
Notes: Important for later reconciliation with payment enums

RawBacklogText: Law enumerates mandatory fiscal receipt elements at the legal level including taxpayer identity location time identification and verification-related receipt content.
Notes: Needs second-pass mapping against technical and rulebook-level receipt fields

RawBacklogText: Electronic fiscal device may be composed of ESIR one or more PFR components and a secure element; when only the online processor is used a backup processor at the sales location is required, and the device must support automatic stop functionality via the secure element.
Notes: High-value architecture source for ESIR LPFR V-PFR modeling

RawBacklogText: Receipt data is delivered to the Tax Administration in real time when connectivity exists and otherwise must be delivered later, with the legal offline catch-up maximum defined as five days.
Notes: Foundational offline rule for journal and resend logic

CandidateLabel: Receipt kinds and non-fiscal document variants
RawBacklogText: Rulebook distinguishes invoices for retail turnover and advance payments and also non-fiscal documents such as copy training slip and proforma, with visual distinction legends and formatting obligations.
Notes: Key source for invoice/document taxonomy

RawBacklogText: Special rulebook section covers corporate card usage and the possibility of refund scenarios tied to that buyer-identification context.
Notes: Bridge between payment mode and buyer identification

RawBacklogText: Rulebook defines transaction-type families for sale and refund or cancellation behavior.
Notes: Second pass should normalize exact labels and refund/cancel semantics

RawBacklogText: GTIN on invoice items is optional according to the rulebook.
Notes: Simple but important item-field rule

RawBacklogText: Applied tax rates and tax-category data must follow the values obtained from the Tax Administration system rather than arbitrary local definitions.
Notes: Useful to separate domain data from local POS configuration

RawBacklogText: Rulebook enumerates payment methods and adds context-specific restrictions for certain on-site food or bakery flows plus allocation rules for mixed payments.
Notes: Needs exact second-pass extraction of labels and constraints

RawBacklogText: Receipt date and time format is prescribed as dd.mm.yyyy HH:mm:ss.
Notes: Concrete formatting validator

RawBacklogText: Rulebook prescribes counters and uniqueness behavior for invoice numbering at the fiscal receipt level.
Notes: Should reconcile with status lastInvoiceNumber semantics later

RawBacklogText: Rulebook prescribes a QR code or verification hyperlink on the receipt together with a minimum size requirement around 40x40 mm and related presentation constraints.
Notes: Needs reconciliation with technical-guide rendering requirements

RawBacklogText: Rulebook defines when referent document number and related reference data are used across refund cancellation and linked-document scenarios.
Notes: High-value for refund and copy flows

CandidateLabel: Secure element purpose and mandatory use
RawBacklogText: Security element is issued by the Tax Administration for taxpayer authentication invoice signing data-integrity protection and receipt verification; a receipt issued without using the security element cannot be treated as a fiscal receipt.
Notes: Foundational BE obligation

RawBacklogText: Security element maintains counters after each transaction for unique invoice numbering by invoice kind and transaction type and for turnover and tax totals by tax rates and overall amounts.
Notes: Important for audit and amount-status modeling

RawBacklogText: Security element may exist as smart card smart SD card USB token or protected file such as PFX.
Notes: Useful for adapter and deployment taxonomy

CandidateLabel: Certificate signs invoices and reproduces protected password
RawBacklogText: Electronic certificate inside the security element reproduces the taxpayer digital signature for every fiscal invoice and reproduces the protected taxpayer password when connecting to the Tax Administration fiscalization system.
Notes: Bridge between certificate usage and SUF connectivity

RawBacklogText: Security element is issued on taxpayer application containing technical specification pickup method and quantity; the Tax Administration generates it within seven days and keeps issuance records.
Notes: Operational but important for provisioning assumptions

RawBacklogText: Security element certificate validity is time-limited from at least one year to at most four years, renewal can be requested within thirty days before expiry, and theft damage or destruction must be reported within three days for deactivation.
Notes: Important lifecycle rule set

RawBacklogText: Technical guide restates and operationalizes the core concepts of EFU SUF PFR ESIR secure element electronic signature supplier business location and payment.
Notes: Lower authority than law but useful because it frames implementation responsibilities

CandidateLabel: EFD composition readiness and allowed topologies
RawBacklogText: EFU consists of one PFR one or more ESIR instances and a security element; it is considered ready only when approved components are successfully connected to SUF, and the guide lists multiple allowed local and virtual deployment topologies including ERP and web-shop scenarios.
Notes: Strong architecture source for modular ESIR LPFR VPFR design

RawBacklogText: Guide states that the security element is personalized per taxpayer and location, signs receipts, reproduces protected credentials, is independent from ESIR and LPFR suppliers, can be issued for testing but not production use, can be deactivated from SUF, auto-locks when configured limits are exceeded until SUF unlocks it, and communicates with PFR through a defined protocol.
Notes: High-signal operational complement to the rulebook

RawBacklogText: PFR receives transaction data from ESIR analyzes and fiscalizes it calculates tax maintains time and calendar communicates with the security element stores transaction data and transfers fiscal data to SUF; it cannot operate without a security element.
Notes: Good functional decomposition source

RawBacklogText: Virtual PFR is a Tax Administration internet service authenticated with locally installed digital certificates, requires internet connectivity, exposes a unique public address, may fail over between active instances, embeds the security element, and returns time date and tax totals to ESIR.
Notes: Important when we later derive generic PFR contracts

RawBacklogText: Local PFR may be hardware or software integrated or independent from ESIR, communicates with ESIR SUF and the security element using ISO/IEC 7816-4, stores encrypted unsent data, supports both internet and local reading, processes SUF commands sequentially, must work offline for fiscalization, must expose an external interface for tax-officer download, and must show current state to the operator.
Notes: Very strong source for LPFR adapter surface and operational rules

RawBacklogText: ESIR may issue electronic or printed receipts, receives fiscal data including QR or verification hyperlink from PFR, communicates with LPFR over HTTP and with VPFR over HTTPS.
Notes: Direct transport-layer design input

RawBacklogText: Receipt creation is explicitly a two-phase flow: ESIR sends a request with invoice kind transaction type payment item and buyer-taxpayer data, and PFR returns a response containing taxpayer identity signed timestamp sequential number digital signature and shareable security-element data.
Notes: Good source for abstract contract narrative beyond raw API spec

RawBacklogText: Guide shows fiscalization exchange steps such as QR generation encryption key generation and readout storage, and states every issued fiscal receipt must be verifiable via SUF immediately after issuance regardless of whether PFR has already delivered it to SUF.
Notes: High-value rule for offline and public verification expectations

RawBacklogText: Technical guide lists mandatory receipt content including invoice and transaction kinds taxpayer identity buyer PIB when applicable item lines tax specifications totals payment and refund amounts timestamp serial numbers QR or hyperlink and referent document number when needed.
Notes: Strong raw field cluster source

CandidateLabel: Receipt kinds transaction types and payment methods
RawBacklogText: Guide defines invoice kinds turnover and advance plus non-fiscal documents copy training and proforma, transaction types sale and refund or cancellation, named payment methods, and mixed-payment disclosure requirements.
Notes: Useful triangulation source alongside the rulebook

RawBacklogText: Copy training and proforma documents must visibly contain the legends KOPIJA OBUKA PREDRACUN and OVO NIJE FISKALNI RACUN in text that cannot be changed or removed and is at least double the size of other text.
Notes: Reinforces rendering obligations from the rulebook

CandidateLabel: Separate counters QR verification referent docs and advertisement font
RawBacklogText: Guide requires separate per-kind and per-transaction counters, prescribes QR constraints and verification modes, defines referent document use cases for refund advance copy and proforma-related flows, and limits advertisements to a different smaller font.
ExistingWorkRefs: Invoice numbering; QR rules; ReferentDocument; advertising text rule
Notes: Dense rendering and linking rule set

RawBacklogText: Guide defines supplier approval workflow for ESIR and LPFR, mandatory resubmission on changes, published approved versions, revocation on non-compliance, access to automated LPFR tests and test security elements, and the finding categories non-compliance defect suspicion observation and improvement.
Notes: Important for later separation of stable domain vs approval-sensitive behavior

RawBacklogText: Manual ESIR testing requires product brochure user manual installation instructions and configuration guidance covering supplier information versioning security warnings setup of tax parameters connection to LPFR or VPFR and security-element insertion.
Notes: Approval-facing but valuable for later implementation-facing docs

CandidateLabel: ESIR cannot issue without PFR mandatory data
RawBacklogText: Manual test requires ESIR to refuse issuing receipts when disconnected from LPFR or VPFR or when the security element or certificate is unavailable and to notify the user that the receipt cannot be issued.
Notes: Strong behavioral guardrail

CandidateLabel: ESIR must preserve mandatory receipt data and formatting
RawBacklogText: Manual test checks header data cashier identification buyerId optional buyer field ESIR number format referent number and referent time item list tax data payment type PFR time counters QR scanability line framing and the non-fiscal legend; it also states ESIR time must be absent except for certain advance-sale bank-transfer cases.
ExistingWorkRefs: RUL-090; Q-014; QR rules; ESIR time rule
Notes: Dense source for rendering and presence validators

RawBacklogText: On startup ESIR must authenticate with PFR using either PFX on ESIR or smart card on LPFR together with valid PIN or PAC credentials and may require manual connection establishment if auto-connect fails.
Notes: Useful when deriving operational contracts around startup and connectivity

RawBacklogText: Manual ESIR testing includes optional pre-issuance removal of items while leaving at least one item and support for item-level discounts with the reduced final price recorded by PFR.
Notes: Optional product-surface features but still part of observed expectations

CandidateLabel: All payment methods and repeated mixed payments
RawBacklogText: ESIR must support every payment method from the technical guide and may repeat the same payment method more than once on a single mixed-payment receipt.
Notes: Useful for second-pass payment validator inventory

RawBacklogText: ESIR must allow GTIN entry and make it visible in the administration portal, must require referent number for refund and copy receipts, and must allow referent number on turnover sale when closing an advance-refund flow.
Notes: Bridges UI behavior with fiscal reference flows

CandidateLabel: Journal search item catalog and quantity behavior
RawBacklogText: Manual test expects an electronic journal with search, ability to add new items, change prices, choose quantities with three decimals, and add items either by name or by GTIN barcode input.
Notes: Operational ESIR capability cluster

RawBacklogText: ESIR must round item prices and totals to at least two decimals using standard threshold rounding where third decimal below five rounds down and five or above rounds up, with explicit numeric examples for quantities and totals.
Notes: Concrete numeric validator source

RawBacklogText: Manual test requires ESIR to print or display current tax labels and rates, show active taxes on command rounded to two decimals, rely only on tax rates exposed by PFR or SUF, and reject issuance when a non-existent tax rate is entered.
ExistingWorkRefs: RUL tax rates from PFR; validation error expectations
Notes: Very strong validator source

CandidateLabel: Electronic delivery for all supported receipt types
RawBacklogText: Where printing is not possible such as online sales, ESIR must support electronic delivery for every receipt type it supports over a valid channel such as email SMS or compatible messaging app while preserving all mandatory receipt data.
Notes: Directly relevant for later output-adapter design

CandidateLabel: Required sample-receipt set and referent-time fallback note
RawBacklogText: Manual review of receipt examples requires multi-item multi-tax decimal-price change-making and both LPFR and VPFR scenarios; it also notes that refund flows without referent time may fall back to current PFR date-time and active tax rates which can matter when historical tax rates changed.
Notes: Important approval and validator nuance

CandidateLabel: ESIR time mandatory only for aged bank-transfer advance sale
RawBacklogText: ESIR time is mandatory only when the buyer paid by bank transfer and the seller received that payment recorded on a later day than the eventual advance-sale receipt issuance.
Notes: Narrow but critical conditional field rule

CandidateLabel: Common ESIR mistakes and mandatory supplier sample set
RawBacklogText: Common failures include misplaced optional buyer field missing buyerId missing non-fiscal legend wrong ESIR number wrong refund-copy reference missing buyer-signature line on copy refund and unreadable QR; supplier submission must include sample receipts for LPFR or VPFR all payment methods multiple tax labels GTIN optional buyer field and rounding scenarios.
ExistingWorkRefs: Open questions and approval sample coverage
Notes: High-value consolidation source for validator backlog

RawBacklogText: Overview defines high-level principles for the fiscalization system including full taxpayer scope phased rollout PKI under tax-authority control online and offline issuing instant public verifiability adaptation of existing invoicing systems separation into invoicing system processor and secure element and open vendor competition.
Notes: Background source but useful for intent behind modular architecture

RawBacklogText: Document-level design principles require sufficient transactional data on payment documents electronic-signature safeguarding by an associated secure element independence of the secure element from the invoicing-system vendor immediate integrity verification unified secure-element inspection methods human-readable journal records and always-available verification services for both inspectors and the public.
Notes: Strong conceptual source for why journal and verification stay first-class in the design

CandidateLabel: Electronic signature protects purchaser identity in B2B
RawBacklogText: In business-to-business transactions an electronic signature must safeguard the purchasing party unique identity from modifications.
Notes: Background principle that supports buyer-identification handling

CandidateLabel: Buyer identification fields and textual rendering examples
RawBacklogText: Codebook defines buyerId as the buyer identification field and buyerCostCenterId as the optional buyer field and shows concrete request plus textual-receipt examples for values such as 10:Pib 12:Pib:Jbkjs and 30:DocumentNumber.
Notes: Good raw source for both schema and textual output

RawBacklogText: Codebook enumerates buyerId prefixes 10 11 12 13 14 15 16 20 21 22 23 30 31 32 33 34 35 36 and 40 together with their semantic meaning such as domestic PIB JMBG JBKJS pension card refugee ID passport foreign IDs and foreign TIN.
Notes: Primary source for buyerId semantics

RawBacklogText: Codebook enumerates optional buyer field prefixes 20 21 30 31 32 33 50 and 60 with meanings such as VAT-form document numbers corporation-card number and refund period for corporate-card reversals.
Notes: Primary source for optional buyer-field semantics

RawBacklogText: Optional buyer field code 60 is explicitly formatted as 60:ddmmgggg_ddmmgggg and applies to turnover refund receipts used to reverse previously recorded corporate-card turnover for a calendar month.
Notes: This is the strongest explicit regex-like format in the codebook

CandidateLabel: Taxpayer obligation scope and QR baseline
RawBacklogText: Guidance states that eFiscalization means issuing a fiscal receipt with a QR code at the moment of retail turnover or received advance, repeats the retail-scope boundary, and emphasizes that every covered transaction must be recorded regardless of payment method.
Notes: Mostly lower-authority restatement but useful as taxpayer-facing wording

CandidateLabel: Guidance receipt content and issuance modes
RawBacklogText: Taxpayer obligations guide repeats mandatory receipt content, states printed receipts carry QR while internet-only sales use verification hyperlink in electronic form, permits self-service electronic issue with hyperlink, and requires Serbian language on receipts regardless of issuance mode.
Notes: Contains a language requirement not yet prominent elsewhere in the backlog

CandidateLabel: PGJO registration model and change deadlines
RawBacklogText: Guide details PGJO registration before using the fiscal device, lists business-space types fixed mobile distance-trade and automat, enumerates PGJO data fields including geolocation and area, and sets change-reporting deadlines of 24 hours before planned change or 24 hours after learning about force-majeure changes.
Notes: Strong operational source for business-location administration

CandidateLabel: Vacation suspension and permanent deregistration flow
RawBacklogText: Guide gives the two-step PGJO process for temporary closure during vacation using fields 2.12 and 2.13 and explains permanent deregistration plus the follow-up EP IPP filing when retail activity stops in a space but business activity continues there.
Notes: Useful for administrative adapter surfaces and operator workflows

CandidateLabel: Security element issuance usage return and revocation reasons
RawBacklogText: Guide explains BE purposes, mandatory use, forms currently issued by the Tax Authority, ESF prerequisite after PGJO, issued-data fields, seven-day issuance, expiry and renewal timing, three-day theft-or-damage reporting, and return or revocation reasons including forgotten PIN blocked certificate business closure and wrong card format.
Notes: Good taxpayer-facing operational complement to the normative rulebook

CandidateLabel: Advance-payment operational flow and mandatory fields
RawBacklogText: Guide says advance receipts use invoice kind advance sale or advance refund, every subsequent advance sale references the previous one, final turnover sale is preceded by advance refund, advance refund is not issued to the buyer, the advertisement area becomes mandatory for certain advance-closing flows, ESIR time stores the original payment date for aged bank-transfer advances, and a named advance-item table 10-13 is provided for advance recording.
ExistingWorkRefs: Advance flow rules; ESIR time; item naming
Notes: Very dense source of practical advance rules

RawBacklogText: Turnover without consideration that is legally treated like turnover for consideration is separately recorded by prefixing item name with 00: and using payment type other noncash payment; small-value samples and advertising material that are not considered turnover are excluded.
Notes: Practical encoding rule likely to become a validator

RawBacklogText: Guide repeats referent-number obligations for refund receipts turnover after advances copy receipts and preinvoice-linked turnover or advance receipts, explicitly naming the PFR number as the reference target.
Notes: Useful triangulation source with taxpayer-facing terminology

RawBacklogText: For erroneous sale or advance-sale receipts the seller cancels by issuing refund with the seller own PIB in buyer identification plus the wrong-receipt reference and full copied item set; if buyer identification was omitted it may only be corrected by immediate cancellation and reissue, otherwise a later reissued buyer-identified receipt is invalid.
Notes: High-value operational rule set

RawBacklogText: Sale of a single-purpose voucher is recorded as a turnover sale with a voucher item per VAT rate, only one voucher rate may appear on one receipt, voucher redemption does not generate a fiscal receipt, and refund of the voucher value is recorded back through the voucher item with the gross amount returned.
Notes: Practical domain rule set

RawBacklogText: Guide requires every receipt to be fiscalized through PFR and BE, sent in real time or within five days after connectivity resumes, stored in internal memory until delivery, optionally supplied by buyer verification or by tax-office readout on BE return, and kept only until ESF validates transmission and generates proof of readout.
ExistingWorkRefs: Offline delivery window; proof of audit/readout
Notes: Important operational cluster for journal and resend logic

RawBacklogText: Guide notes there is no obligation for daily or periodic printed reports in the new model, on-site cash refund requires buyer identification from an allowed document but not JMBG, card or other noncash refund needs only the customer receipt number, diplomatic and international-organization sales use PIB or diplomatic ID plus form numbers, and even a business that normally does not retail must fiscalize sale of a fixed asset to a natural person.
ExistingWorkRefs: No daily reports; noncash refund; diplomatic sales
Notes: Contains several practical guidance nuances that often surface in implementation questions

SourcePath: knowledge_base/Пореска управа __ Одговори на најчешћa питања.html
CandidateLabel: FAQ PFR topology and offline baseline
RawBacklogText: FAQ states PFR may be local or virtual, a taxpayer using only the virtual processor in the tax network must still secure at least one local processor per physical sales location, and offline receipt issuing is possible except for pure online-sale scenarios because LPFR stores and signs transactions until connectivity returns.
Notes: Practical restatement of the architecture requirement

SourcePath: knowledge_base/Пореска управа __ Одговори на најчешћa питања.html
CandidateLabel: FAQ security-element acquisition expiry and ESF revocation flow
RawBacklogText: FAQ explains BE acquisition through ePorezi and ESF, file delivery to the tax mailbox, certificate lifetime one to four years, three-day damage or loss notice, and ESF revocation flow identified by JID with user-visible statuses and reasons such as forgotten PIN or PAK blocked certificate lost card suspected PFX compromise and business closure.
Notes: Adds user-journey detail not present in the normative texts

SourcePath: knowledge_base/Пореска управа __ Одговори на најчешћa питања.html
RawBacklogText: FAQ repeats the seven payment labels, confirms mixed-payment receipts must show multiple payment methods with split amounts, and states public verification remains available for ten years after receipt issuance and can be repeated multiple times with internet-connected QR or hyperlink access.
Notes: Ten-year verification horizon is useful to preserve

SourcePath: knowledge_base/Пореска управа __ Одговори на најчешћa питања.html
CandidateLabel: FAQ installments advance references online-only electronic issue and print default
RawBacklogText: FAQ says a fiscal receipt and the tax obligation cannot be split into installments, advance-linked turnover references advance receipts, receipts may be issued offline with at least one LPFR per sales point, online-only retail issues electronic receipts with hyperlink, and fixed-store retail prints by default while electronic delivery is allowed with buyer consent.

CandidateLabel: SE Sign Invoice sample purpose and prerequisites
RawBacklogText: Both legacy and v3 SESignInvoice samples are minimal demonstration apps for generating and sending APDU commands to the TaxCore Secure Element and require an operational smart-card reader plus an initialized smart card with the Secure Element applet, with guidance to configure PIN and use only one reader.
Notes: Useful as reference-sample context rather than normative behavior

RawBacklogText: Legacy sample constructs a 68-byte SignInvoice payload composed of 8-byte timestamp 20-byte taxpayerId 20-byte buyerId 1-byte invoiceType 1-byte transactionType 8-byte amount 1-byte category count 1-byte tax category and 8-byte tax-category amount with examples normal sale and VAT category 3.
Notes: Good raw payload-layout evidence

CandidateLabel: V3 SignInvoice payload layout and field-size change
RawBacklogText: V3 sample reduces SignInvoice payload to 66 bytes by using 7-byte big-endian encoded invoice amount and tax-category amount instead of the legacy 8-byte representation while preserving the same logical field order.
Notes: Important version-delta evidence for APDU adapters

RawBacklogText: Sample loops through connected readers, selects the TaxCore applet AID A000000748464A492D546178436F7265, verifies PIN with CLA 0x88 INS 0x11 P1 0x04 P2 0x00 using digit-array PIN data, and sends SignInvoice with CLA 0x88 INS 0x13 P1 0x04 P2 0x00 over extended APDU.
Notes: Reference implementation evidence for low-level adapter behavior

RawBacklogText: GET /api/v3/environment-parameters returns environment metadata such as organizationName serverTimeZone address endpoints environmentName logo ntpServer and supportedLanguages; response fields may grow over time.
Notes: Explicit official contract added during reconciliation seeding

RawBacklogText: GET /api/v3/attention probes E-SDC availability before VerifyPin or CreateInvoice and succeeds only when the next sensitive command can be sent immediately.
Notes: Explicit official contract added during reconciliation seeding

RawBacklogText: POST /api/v3/invoices is the main invoice-signing contract for V-SDC and E-SDC; it accepts JSON InvoiceRequest, optional RequestId and Accept-Language headers, and optional PAC for V-SDC.
Notes: Explicit contract row split out from the detailed Create Invoice section

RawBacklogText: Select SE Applet APDU routes subsequent secure-element commands to the TaxCore applet using CLA 0x00 INS 0xA4 P1P2 0x0400 and AID A000000748464A492D546178436F726500.
Notes: Explicit official APDU selection contract

RawBacklogText: Get Secure Element Version returns the SE API version as 12 bytes made of three unsigned 4-byte integers for major minor and patch, using CLA 0x88 INS 0x08.
Notes: Explicit official APDU version contract

RawBacklogText: Forward Secure Element Directive sends a TaxCore-provided 512-byte directive payload to the Secure Element using CLA 0x88 INS 0x40 with version-gated CRC support from SE 3.2.5 onward.
Notes: Explicit official APDU forwarding contract

RawBacklogText: Export Certificate APDU exports the taxpayer certificate from the Secure Element in DER format using CLA 0x88 INS 0x04 and is the basis for deriving UID TIN and location metadata.
Notes: Official secure-element certificate export contract

RawBacklogText: Get Last Signed Invoice APDU returns the same response-data structure as Sign Invoice and supports optional CRC from SE 3.2.5 onward, using CLA 0x88 INS 0x15.
Notes: This is the secure-element APDU contract and must be kept separate from HTTP invoice recovery

RawBacklogText: Get PIN tries left from SE Applet returns remaining secure-element PIN attempts before lockout using CLA 0x88 INS 0x16 and a one-byte response payload.
Notes: New secure-element auxiliary contract discovered during reconciliation

RawBacklogText: Get CertParams returns UID plus certificate NotBefore and NotAfter in UTC Unix timestamp form using CLA 0x88 INS 0x33 and is available from SE 3.2.8 onward.
Notes: Explicit official APDU certificate-parameter contract

RawBacklogText: Select PKI Applet routes subsequent PKI commands to the smart-card PKI applet using CLA 0x00 INS 0xA4 and one of two AIDs depending on whether the SE version is below 3.1.1 or at least 3.1.1.
Notes: Explicit official PKI applet selection contract

RawBacklogText: Sign Invoice APDU signs invoice payload bytes and returns fiscalization data using CLA 0x88 INS 0x13, with version-gated CRC behavior and a certificate-validity time window requirement from SE 3.2.8 onward.
Notes: Explicit official APDU fiscalization contract

RawBacklogText: Amount Status returns the current SALE+REFUND amount and the secure-element limit as two 7-byte values using CLA 0x88 INS 0x14.
Notes: Explicit official APDU amount-status contract

RawBacklogText: Export TaxCore Public Key returns a 259-byte public-key structure used to encrypt audit packages, via CLA 0x88 INS 0x07.
Notes: New audit APDU contract discovered during reconciliation

RawBacklogText: Export Audit Data returns encrypted audit data with version-gated optional CRC using CLA 0x88 INS 0x12 and supersedes the older Export Internal Data command naming in reference code.
Notes: Official audit export contract that supersedes the older reference-only label

RawBacklogText: Start Audit notifies the Secure Element that audit has started and returns an encrypted auditRequestPayload for TaxCore, using CLA 0x88 INS 0x21 with optional CRC from SE 3.2.5 onward.
Notes: New audit-session contract discovered during reconciliation

RawBacklogText: End Audit finalizes the audit process in the Secure Element using CLA 0x88 INS 0x20 with a 256-byte TaxCore payload and optional CRC from SE 3.2.5 onward.
Notes: Official audit-finalization contract corresponding to the reference FinishAudit naming
